title: pyrus aucuparia
intro: Pyrus aucuparia, also known as Rowan or Mountain Ash, is a deciduous tree or shrub native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its clusters of small white flowers and bright red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are believed to help protect against infections and promote wound healing. The Rowan tree is said to offer several health benefits, including reducing the risk of heart disease, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Rowan, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems.
